summary: Relicense to dual MIT OR Apache-2.0 — add LICENSE-MIT + LICENSE-APACHE and set the Cargo.toml `license` field, before external contributors

per.section: Decision: the license is MIT OR Apache-2.0, dual, before launch

new.description: The standard Apache-2.0 license text (the patent grant the dual choice buys).

edit.description: Set `license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"` (the SPDX expression the dual grant requires; the API-guidelines / uv / Biome choice), replacing the current `license = "MIT"` (Cargo.toml:6). The `license` field follows the LICENSE change, per the Decision.

acceptance: The repo carries LICENSE-MIT + LICENSE-APACHE and Cargo.toml declares `license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"`; cargo build/fmt/clippy green.
notes: Relicense now — the Decision's window is 'no external contributors' (clean trunk confirms). Verified on disk this tick: no bare LICENSE file; sole declaration is Cargo.toml `license = "MIT"` (line 6), flipped to SPDX — nothing to retire. Disjoint: owns Cargo.toml `license` + LICENSE-* files, touches no source/README.

summary: Author AGENTS.md at root — contributor-facing build/test/lint + architecture map, pointing to the AI-authorship policy; CLAUDE.md stays canonical and untouched

per.section: The front door — the README is the landing page

new.description: The 2025-26 agent-readable contributor doc at repo root: build/test/lint commands (cargo build/test, clippy -D warnings, fmt), a short architecture map of `src/` (the contract engine, kinds, surface, drift), and a pointer to the AI-contribution policy in `.github/CONTRIBUTING.md` (verified on disk). Per the RESOLVED (launch-front-door-docs) fork it is a SEPARATE contributor doc — it does NOT touch `.claude/**` or `CLAUDE.md`, and CLAUDE.md is NOT gutted to source it (the ruff/uv 'CLAUDE.md = @AGENTS.md' move is rejected here; CLAUDE.md is the pipeline's operating harness, the recursive-dogfood fixture).

acceptance: AGENTS.md exists at root as a contributor-facing doc (build/test/lint + architecture map + pointer to the AI-authorship policy); CLAUDE.md and `.claude/**` are untouched; cargo green.
notes: Fileable per RESOLVED (launch-front-door-docs). Root doc only, disjoint from every other entry (owns AGENTS.md alone). The chore(harness) boundary holds: this entry must not edit CLAUDE.md or `.claude/**`. CONTRIBUTING.md lives at `.github/CONTRIBUTING.md` (verified on disk), so link there.

summary: Add the `why <member>` and `requirements [<name>]` read verbs — read-only traversals over the coverage + graph data `check` already computes, never gating

per.section: Decision: the CLI gains a read family — `why` and `requirements`

new.description: The read family: `why(member)` walks the requirement↔`satisfies` edge forward (the requirements this member fills with their authored rationale, the package its kind binds, its declared edges in/out); `requirements(name?)` walks it in reverse (the roster with per-requirement satisfier set + coverage state; with a name, that requirement's satisfiers and the blast radius a removal would strand). Both are projections over data `check` already computes (coverage::check, graph::edges) — no new engine semantics, no non-zero exit. Output is a teaching surface: full sentences in the corpus's exact vocabulary (specs/50-distribution.md, 'the gate teaches'), not a table dump.

Insta-snapshot proofs over a fixture surface: `why` narrates a member's filled requirements + edges; `requirements` lists the roster with coverage state and, for one name, its satisfier set + removal blast radius. Assert both always exit zero (never a gate).

edit.description: Add `pub mod read;` beside the other modules (this entry owns the line).

Add `Command::Why { member }` and `Command::Requirements { name: Option<String> }` to the clap enum plus their dispatch arms, each mapping the read-module result to stdout and exit 0. Thin dispatch only — the traversal logic lives in `src/read.rs` (.claude/rules/rust.md).

tests.asserts: `why <member>` renders the requirements it satisfies (with rationale) + governing package + edges; `requirements [<name>]` renders the roster/coverage forward and the satisfier set + removal blast radius in reverse; both exit zero on all inputs.

acceptance: `temper why <member>` and `temper requirements [<name>]` traverse the requirement↔`satisfies` edge (forward / reverse) over data `check` computes, narrate in the corpus's vocabulary, and never gate (always exit 0); cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: Fileable per RESOLVED (read-verbs) — the surface-language migration + coverage/graph data have landed (verified: coverage.rs, graph.rs live; no `why`/`requirements` in main.rs). Disjoint: owns src/read.rs + tests/read_verbs.rs, plus its own main.rs/lib.rs lines. Serializes only against a revived AGENT-KIND (also main.rs), which is deferred.

summary: Add the `section_contains {heading, marker}` structural predicate + its `## Decision`-block extraction primitive — the decisions-name-alternatives capability the spec package awaits

new.description: Fixture-driven proof of the new predicate: a spec whose every `## Decision` section carries the `Rejected` marker passes; one with a marker-less Decision section fires exactly one diagnostic; a clean fixture trips nothing. Proves the predicate is decidable (a true positive) over the new section-body extraction.

edit.description: Add a section-body extraction primitive (the `## Decision`-block — heading + its body — the module docstring already flags as waiting on this fork) to the closed `Primitive` vocabulary, yielding a named sections feature; keep out-of-vocabulary primitives rejected at load.
Add the extractor that yields ATX sections (heading + body span) into `Features`, delegating to the same body-parsing logic the built-in projectors use so a composed sections read is byte-identical.

Add `Predicate::SectionContains { heading, marker }` to the closed vocabulary with its `key`/`target` (`section_contains`, targeting `heading`+`marker`) and its parse, rejecting unknown keys as elsewhere. Enumerated in the spec's structural primitives (specs/10-contracts.md).

Decide `section_contains` over the extracted sections feature: every section whose heading starts with the declared `heading` text must carry the declared `marker`; emit a diagnostic per bare section. Decidable, tier-1.

tests.asserts: the `section_contains` predicate fires on a Decision section missing its `Rejected` marker and stays silent when every matching section carries it; an out-of-vocabulary primitive/predicate is still rejected at load.

acceptance: The closed vocabulary carries `section_contains {heading, marker}` decided over a new `## Decision`-block/section extraction primitive; proven over fixtures (fires on a bare Decision section, silent on a clean one); cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: Fileable per RESOLVED (decision-marker-predicate) — the spec kind's package exists (.temper/packages/spec/PACKAGE.md), which explicitly notes decisions-name-alternatives is 'not yet in the engine'. This entry ships the engine capability over test fixtures; adopting the clause into temper's own spec package (dogfood) is a follow-on chore, not this entry. Disjoint: owns kind/extract/contract/engine + tests/section_contains.rs; shares no file with READ-VERBS.

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind — `.claude/agents/*.md` imported as one AGENT.md member document, checked by an authored agent package

gate.reason: the requirements/satisfies reframe deprioritizes more built-in kinds; the whole surface-language chain it waited on has shipped, so the blocker is priority alone — revive only if a story demands the agent kind

new.description: The Agent IR — the typed model for a Claude Code subagent at `.claude/agents/<name>.md`, mirroring `src/rule.rs`: typed YAML frontmatter on the projection side (`name`, `description`, optional `tools`/`model`), unknown keys preserved, content-faithful body, provenance. Identity is the path (file stem). The surface member is one AGENT.md document (clause-module header) like every kind.

The agent kind's authored built-in package (clauses + guidance) in the one-document medium, embedded with the std-lib (the EMBED-BUILTIN-PACKAGES model — NOT a contracts/agent.toml). Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]`; `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores; an advisory `max_lines`.
Fixture-driven proof mirroring `tests/rule_contract.rs`: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an insta import round-trip proving idempotence.

edit.description: Add `pub mod agent;` beside the other artifact-kind modules (this entry owns the line).
Extend `Workspace` with `agents` loaded under `<dir>/agents/*` and a `WorkspaceError` arm; update the load tests.
Discover `.claude/agents/*.md`, project each to one `<into>/agents/<name>/AGENT.md` member document, add an `[[agent]]` lock row per agent.
Bind the agent package, wire admissibility + conformance streams and `by_kind` for the kind.

tests.asserts: each decidable clause of the agent package fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the agent package (conformance) and that package passes admissibility; `temper import` projects agents as one-document members idempotently with `[[agent]]` lock rows; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Deferred by the requirements/satisfies reframe: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction. Shaped to the surface-language forms — one AGENT.md member document, an authored PACKAGE.md, no meta.toml, no contracts/agent.toml. On revival its shared-surface blast radius (lib/check/extract/import/main) serializes against any then-open main.rs entry (e.g. READ-VERBS).

summary: Ship acquisition channels — npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install — auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected.

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`, confirmed on disk). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's pattern) plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/50-distribution.md`). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than blindly clobbering the harness manifest. Scoped/prefixed names per the resolved (project-name) fork.

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infra, mostly outside `src/`; the cargo gates can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human creds (npm org, tap, signing). NB: root `package.json` already exists as the *private* flume manifest — reconcile or split, never blindly create a second. Parked until a human sets creds up.
